title: Vet YamlDotNet package per AGENTS.md package policy
description:
## Source
Council review at `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Moderate concern M5  
Blueprint YAML design — Phase 1 dependency

## Background
The blueprint YAML design introduces `YamlDotNet` as a new NuGet dependency. Per AGENTS.md Package Vetting Policy:

- **P-1:** Documented justification required for every new package
- **P-1a:** License whitelist — MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-2/3-Clause only. No GPL/AGPL/copyleft.
- **P-2:** Every dependency must be listed in `WebUI.Blazor/wwwroot/about.html`
- **P-3:** Vulnerable packages are a P0 blocker — `dotnet list package --vulnerable` must return zero results
- **P-4:** Deprecated packages are prohibited
- **P-5:** Direct pinning of transitive deps requires justification and a removal plan

## Scope
1. Add `YamlDotNet` to `Agent.Construction.csproj` (or the appropriate project)
2. Verify license compliance (YamlDotNet is MIT — ✅)
3. Run `dotnet list package --vulnerable` and verify zero results
4. Verify not deprecated (check latest stable version on NuGet)
5. Create package justification document in `Data/Pages/policies/package-justifications/yamldotnet.md`
6. Add to `WebUI.Blazor/wwwroot/about.html`

## Alternate approach
Evaluate whether a hand-rolled YAML frontmatter parser could replace the full YamlDotNet dependency. Blueprint YAML uses a small, known schema — a minimal parser might be simpler and avoid the dependency entirely.

## Deliverables
- Package justification document
- `about.html` update
- Vulnerability scan results (as comment in task)
- Decision record: YamlDotNet vs. hand-rolled parser

## Exit Criteria
- Package meets all 5 policy requirements
- Justification document written and reviewed
- Alternative (hand-rolled) evaluated and decision documented
